frontier_signals.title: Portable skills are becoming reusable agent infrastructure
frontier_signals.claim: Skills are evolving from prompt fragments into explicit reusable infrastructure.
frontier_signals.why_it_matters: Supports promoted capabilities that persist beyond one run.

frontier_signals.title: Delegation boundaries need explicit metadata
frontier_signals.claim: Portable skill metadata and delegation boundaries are an active design problem.
frontier_signals.why_it_matters: Supports adding a promotion queue instead of ad hoc capability notes.

frontier_signals.title: Sessions and tracing are table stakes in agent runtimes
frontier_signals.claim: Durable session and tracing semantics are now baseline expectations.
frontier_signals.why_it_matters: EvolveX already traces live runs, but it still needed a durable cross-run growth ledger.

frontier_signals.title: Persistent HUDs improve operator trust
frontier_signals.claim: Always-visible status, queue state, and review surfaces matter for long-running agent workflows.
frontier_signals.why_it_matters: Supports a VS Code-like operator console instead of a passive dashboard.

growth_artifacts.name: Verified Growth Registry
growth_artifacts.repo_gap: No shipped growth schema or durable promotion substrate existed before this rerun.
growth_artifacts.smallest_test: Append JSONL records, expose a read API, and seed a validated run.

growth_artifacts.name: Fact Check Ledger
growth_artifacts.repo_gap: Docs and product story could drift ahead of shipped routes and branches.
growth_artifacts.smallest_test: Record landed, unsupported, and branch-only claims in a durable bundle.

growth_artifacts.name: Growth HUD and Promotion Queue
growth_artifacts.repo_gap: The dashboard had no cross-run growth surface or actionable promotion review state.
growth_artifacts.smallest_test: Show latest run summary, top candidate, and queue state in the console.

claim_checks.claim: Arena mode is shipped on the current mainline.
claim_checks.source: validated rerun and shipped commit history
claim_checks.notes: Verified in code and in the validated rerun.

claim_checks.claim: Genesis mode is shipped on the current mainline.
claim_checks.source: validated rerun and shipped commit history
claim_checks.notes: Verified in code and in the validated rerun.

claim_checks.claim: Housekeeping endpoints are live on the shipped API surface.
claim_checks.source: stale documentation before the validated rerun
claim_checks.notes: Useful experiment, but not a shipped route on the current API surface.

promotion_candidates.title: Reality Contract / Claim Verifier
promotion_candidates.why_it_matters: Prevents product narrative from outrunning the code contract.
promotion_candidates.evidence: The validated rerun caught stale housekeeping claims before promotion.

promotion_candidates.title: Growth HUD and Promotion Queue
promotion_candidates.why_it_matters: Turns the dashboard into an operator console with actionable promotion state.
promotion_candidates.evidence: Persistent HUD patterns and the validated rerun both point to cross-run review surfaces.
promotion_candidates.required_scrub: Keep the console grounded in shipped APIs and durable records only.

promotion_candidates.title: Verified Growth Registry
promotion_candidates.why_it_matters: Provides the durable substrate for compounding outputs, claim checks, and promotions across runs.
promotion_candidates.evidence: Validated research and local rerun data both converged on the need for a durable registry before more UI or branch salvage work.
promotion_candidates.required_scrub: Keep the seeded artifact set public-safe and repo-relative.
